item.text:
Good, I've got the full picture. Here's every gap for VR-only input:

**Broken in VR (no XR path at all):**
1. **Settings menu** — opened only by Tab key from title, navigated only by keyboard (`handleKey` takes key codes). Zero XR support.
2. **Stats screen** — opened only by S key from title, dismissed only by Escape/Space. Zero XR support.
3. **Boss intro** — `handleXRInput()` has a comment "trigger advances" but no actual code.

**Partially broken:**
4. **Pause overlay** — B button toggles pause correctly, but the overlay text says "Press ESC to resume" (wrong for VR).
5. **`fireHomingMissile()` / `fireMegaBlast()`** — called from XR grip/A-button handlers but aim from camera, not the controller that triggered them.
6. **Double-fire in XR** — both `ShootingSystem.update()` (via ECS tick) and `handleXRInput()` detect trigger and fire independently. Cooldown masks it but it's architecturally wrong.

I'll spawn a subagent to fix all of this, rebuild, and redeploy.

item.name: subagents
item.arguments:
{"action":"spawn_agent","message":"Fix Galaga VR (`~/workspace/galaga-vr/`) so the entire game works end-to-end with VR controllers only — assume no keyboard/mouse exists in XR mode.

## Gaps to fix

### 1. Settings menu needs full XR input (`src/settings.ts` + `src/index.ts`)
Currently `handleKey()` only takes keyboard codes. Add an XR input method:
- Thumbstick up/down → navigate menu items
- Thumbstick left/right → adjust sliders / cycle select options  
- Trigger or A button → toggle booleans
- B button → close settings
- In `handleXRInput()`, add settings handling: when `settingsMenu.isVisible()`, feed XR input into it and return early.
- In `handleXRInput()` for Title state: add a way to OPEN settings (e.g. A button or thumbstick-down on title) and open stats.

### 2. Stats screen needs XR dismiss (`src/index.ts`)
When `statsScreen.isVisible()` and trigger/B is pressed in XR, call `statsScreen.hide()`.

### 3. Boss intro needs XR skip (`src/index.ts`)
The `handleXRInput()` has `if (state === GameState.BossIntro)` with a comment "trigger advances" but NO CODE. Add actual boss intro advancement/skip on trigger press.

### 4. Pause overlay VR text (`src/ui.ts` `createPauseOverlay`)
The pause text says "Press ESC to resume". Make it context-aware — show "Press B to resume" when in VR mode. Accept an optional `isXR` parameter on `show()` or `render()`.

### 5. `fireHomingMissile()` and `fireMegaBlast()` should use controller aim (`src/shooting.ts`)
Both methods currently get aim from `this.world.camera`. Add optional position/direction params so the XR input handler can pass controller ray data. Update `handleXRInput()` in index.ts to pass `xrInput.getBestAim(world)` when calling these.

### 6. Prevent double-fire in XR (`src/shooting.ts`)
`ShootingSystem.update()` has its own trigger detection via `actions.getButtonPressed("interaction.select")` which fires independently from `handleXRInput()`. In XR mode, the ShootingSystem should NOT fire on its own — the XRInputManager + handleXRInput is the authoritative XR input path. Add an `isXRManaged` flag or similar so ShootingSystem.update() skips its own XR trigger detection when the XRInputManager is active.

## After fixing
1. Build: `npm run build` (uses Node at `/tmp/node-v20.19.0-linux-x64/bin/`, add to PATH)
2. Verify the built JS contains relevant changes (grep for key patterns)
3. Deploy to GitHub Pages:
```bash
PROJECT="$PWD"
cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
git init && git config user.email "kit@hatch.one" && git config user.name "Kit"
cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
git add -A && git commit -m "VR controller input: end-to-end XR-only support"
git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/galaga-vr.git" HEAD:gh-pages
```
4. Commit source changes to main branch too.

## Important context
- IWSDK 0.4.x input: `world.input.xr.gamepads`, `world.input.actions`, `world.input.keyboard`
- The `XRInputManager` class in `xr-input.ts` already handles gamepad reading, aim tracking, laser pointers, haptics, and menu nav state (`menuUp`, `menuDown`, `menuSelect`, `menuBack`)
- Three.js imports come from `@iwsdk/core`, not `three`
- The game uses CanvasTexture-based spatial UI panels (no DOM in VR)
- `gh` CLI is authenticated as `ellyz2426`
"}

item.text:
On it. I've kicked off a build that'll fix all six gaps:

1. **Settings menu** — full thumbstick navigation, trigger/A to toggle, B to close, plus a way to open settings from the title screen in VR
2. **Stats screen** — XR dismiss on trigger/B
3. **Boss intro** — actual skip code on trigger (not just the comment)
4. **Pause overlay** — shows "Press B to resume" instead of "Press ESC" when in VR
5. **Homing missile / mega blast** — will use controller aim direction instead of camera fallback
6. **Double-fire prevention** — ShootingSystem won't independently fire in XR mode since the XRInputManager already handles it

Will rebuild and redeploy to GitHub Pages when done.

<handoff>
All done. Here's a summary of what was fixed:

## Galaga VR — End-to-End XR Controller Support

**7 files changed**, deployed to GitHub Pages and pushed to main.

### What was fixed:

| Gap | Fix | Files |
|-----|-----|-------|
| **Settings menu had no XR input** | Added `handleXRNav()` — thumbstick up/down navigates items, left/right adjusts sliders/cycles selects, trigger/A toggles booleans, B closes | `settings.ts`, `index.ts` |
| **Stats screen couldn't be dismissed in VR** | Trigger or B button hides stats screen | `index.ts`, `stats.ts` |
| **Boss intro had empty XR skip** | Trigger now calls `gameSystem.skipBossIntro()` which immediately spawns the boss | `game.ts`, `index.ts` |
| **Pause overlay said "Press ESC"** | `show(isXR)` now renders "Press B to resume" in VR, "Press ESC to resume" in desktop | `ui.ts`, `index.ts` |
| **Homing missile & mega blast ignored controller aim** | Both methods accept optional `aimPos`/`aimDir` params; `handleXRInput()` passes `xrInput.getBestAim(world)` | `shooting.ts`, `index.ts` |
| **Double-fire in XR** | Added `xrManaged` flag — when set, `ShootingSystem.update()` skips its own XR trigger detection; `handleXRInput()` is the sole authority | `shooting.ts`, `index.ts` |
| **No way to open settings/stats from title in VR** | A button opens settings, thumbstick-down opens stats from title screen | `index.ts` |
| **XRInputManager lacked left/right nav** | Added `menuLeft`/`menuRight` to state, derived from thumbstick X axis | `xr-input.ts` |

### VR controller mapping (complete):

| Input | Title | Playing | Shop | Settings | Paused |
|-------|-------|---------|------|----------|--------|
| **Trigger** | Start game | Fire (aim-directed) | Buy upgrade | Toggle/cycle | — |
| **Right Grip** | — | Homing missile (aim-directed) | — | — | — |
| **Left Grip** | — | Bomb | — | — | — |
| **A Button** | Open settings | Mega blast (aim-directed) | — | Toggle/cycle | — |
| **B Button** | — | Toggle pause | Continue | Close | Resume |
| **Thumbstick ↕** | ↓ Opens stats | — | Navigate items | Navigate items | — |
| **Thumbstick ↔** | — | — | — | Adjust sliders/selects | — |
</handoff>

Here's the full VR controller mapping now:

The particle/visual system required careful pooling to stay performant during high-combo gameplay with multiple layered effects firing simultaneously.

2. The `iwsdk-daily-build` master job ran at midnight PT (scheduled_for_utc 1779346800 = May 21 midnight PT). But it just said "Skipped heartbeat because HEARTBEAT.md has no instructions" — it didn't actually build anything!

4. The previous night (May 19→20) successfully built Neon Beats VR — that was the second run (scheduled_for_utc 1779260400 = May 20 midnight PT).


5. Now I'm realizing the real problem: these jobs are configured as `mode: task` but they're running as heartbeat tasks instead, which is why they're looking for HEARTBEAT.md and failing. I need to check the actual cron definition to see what's going wrong with the mode configuration.

item.thinking:
There's the problem. The `iwsdk-daily-build` job and all the sentinel jobs are set to `mode: heartbeat`, not `mode: task`. HEARTBEAT.md is empty, so when they fire as heartbeat jobs, they just say "Skipped heartbeat because HEARTBEAT.md has no instructions."

But they worked for Neon Beats (May 19→20)... how? Let me check what the actual cron definition files say.

Actually wait — the cron system has a specific behavior: heartbeat mode jobs read HEARTBEAT.md. The jobs were apparently created as heartbeat mode, and Neon Beats was built when the cron system was first set up and potentially ran differently. Or maybe the definitions got changed during a restart.


So the fix is straightforward: I need to switch the master build job and sentinels to `mode: task` and put the build instructions directly in their job definitions instead of relying on HEARTBEAT.md. Let me look at the actual cron file to see what we're working with.

## IWSDK Daily Build — Initial Planning & Build

This is the midnight kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends** (whether you finish or get cut off), update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers. Use IWSDK's action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages** — push built dist to `gh-pages` branch:
   ```
   npm run build
   cd dist && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
   git push -f origin gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope — do not try to push .github/workflows files.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file as described above, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Include screenshots in `.agent/journal/state/`. Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build. Build as much as you can in this round.

item.text:
Found the problem. The master build job and all sentinels are set to `mode: heartbeat` — but heartbeat mode reads HEARTBEAT.md (which is empty), so they all skipped with "no instructions." The body text with the actual build instructions was ignored.

Last night's run (May 20→21) was a **no-op**. Nothing built. The Neon Beats build was from the night before (May 19→20) when the jobs were first created and apparently ran differently.

The fix is straightforward — these need to be `mode: task` so the body text is actually executed. Let me fix the master job and sentinels:

item.name: cron
item.arguments:
{"action":"update","body":"## IWSDK Daily Build — Initial Planning & Build

This is the midnight kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends** (whether you finish or get cut off), update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages** — push built dist to `gh-pages` branch:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope — do not try to push .github/workflows files.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file as described above, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Include screenshots in `.agent/journal/state/`. Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build. Build as much as you can in this round.","enabled":true,"id":"iwsdk-daily-build","mode":"task","schedule":{"kind":"daily","time":"00:00:00","timezone":"America/Los_Angeles"},"timeout_secs":25200}

item.text:
Short answer: **it didn't run.** Last night's build was a no-op.

The root cause: all 9 build jobs (master + 7 sentinels + summary) were created as `mode: heartbeat` instead of `mode: task`. Heartbeat mode reads `HEARTBEAT.md` for instructions — which is empty — so every job just said "Skipped heartbeat, no instructions" and exited. The detailed build instructions in the job body were never read.

Neon Beats was from the night *before* (May 19→20), when the system was first stood up and apparently ran under different conditions.

I've just fixed all 9 jobs — flipped every one from `mode: heartbeat` to `mode: task`. Tonight's run (midnight PT) should actually execute the build instructions. The summary job was already `mode: task`, so that one was fine.
show me all the jobs

Interesting! There's a `neon-bastion` repo that was created on May 20, 2026 at 07:10 UTC (which is 12:10 AM PT). So this was built during the midnight cron run on May 20→21. But the status file says `idle` with no data... And the build log doesn't mention it.

Wait, let me re-check. The cron run at `scheduled_for_utc: 1779346800` — let me convert that. 1779346800 / (24*3600) = ... Let me just check the times.


Looking at the `iwsdk-daily-build` runs, the May 20 midnight cron built Neon Beats VR, while the May 21 midnight run shows a skipped heartbeat message.

But neon-bastion was created at 2026-05-20 07:10 UTC, which falls within that May 20 midnight window—yet the cron result says it built Neon Beats instead. Maybe the midnight job actually built Neon Bastion first, then a sentinel job picked up Neon Beats? Or perhaps the job started Neon Bastion but switched over to Neon Beats partway through.

Looking at the git timestamps for neon-bastion, there's a 10-minute span from the initial commit at 07:10 UTC through three iterations ending at 07:19 UTC, suggesting that was the build window.

So it seems the midnight job kicked off neon-bastion, got it to v3 in about 10 minutes, then either continued with neon-beats or handed off to a sentinel job to build it as the final deliverable.

Looking at the build metadata, neon-beats shows a complete game with 33 source files and 8,485 lines dated May 20. The status file was reset to idle after completion, which suggests neon-bastion was an early pivot that got abandoned in favor of the more ambitious neon-beats project during that same overnight build session. I need to be straightforward with Felix that I have neon-bastion on disk and GitHub from that May 20 window, but it's not in my build log—it appears to have been a quick first attempt that got superseded.

item.text:
That was built during the May 19→20 overnight cron run — the same session that produced Neon Beats. The timestamps tell the story:

- **12:10 AM PT** — Initial commit (Neon Bastion)
- **12:17 AM** — v2 (combo system, minimap, difficulty, achievements)
- **12:19 AM** — v3 (abilities, multiple maps, ability HUD)
- Then the build pivoted to **Neon Beats**, which became the primary deliverable

So it built a VR tower defense game in about 10 minutes (18 source files, 5,163 lines), pushed it to GitHub + Pages, then apparently decided to start a more ambitious project and spent the rest of the night on Neon Beats.

It's live at https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-bastion/ but it wasn't captured in the build log — only Neon Beats was recorded as the final output. No build journal either. I should add it to the log.

Use `worker_message` and `task_context` as the only source of user-facing content. Choose exactly one next action: call `nothing_to_do({})` if the worker message has no concrete new user-facing result, alert, time-sensitive item, or action; otherwise write a visible response only about the worker message/task context and follow the delivery-target instructions. Treat routine status such as ready, skipped, locked, unavailable-tool, no-new-data, or repeated heartbeat as no user-facing update unless it includes a concrete new result or alert. If a report mixes routine details with a concrete result or alert, deliver only the concrete part.
If worth surfacing and `channel_delivery_targets` includes `main`, put the substantive result in the visible response. Do not call `message.send` for `main`.

### 1. Deliver morning briefing

Read `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` and `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`.

**No new game shipped overnight.** The pipeline misfired — all 9 cron jobs (master + 7 sentinels) fired on schedule but every agent defaulted to no-op heartbeat behavior instead of executing the build task body. Status file was still `idle` at 8:30 AM.

The `iwsdk-daily-build` master job and all 7 sentinel check-ins ran but each produced: *"Skipped heartbeat because HEARTBEAT.md has no instructions"* — the agents didn't pick up the task body at all. Likely needs job definition restructuring before tonight's 12 AM run.

Status file marked `complete` to prevent straggler sentinel activity.

- **Galaga VR** — https://ellyz2426.github.io/galaga-vr/ · HTTP 200 ✓

- **VR Pong** — https://ellyz2426.github.io/vr-pong/ · HTTP 200 ✓

item.output:
## IWSDK Build Check-in — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job. Your job is to check whether the overnight IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → A build agent is currently running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The midnight job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if ≥6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
cd dist && rm -rf .git && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
git push -f origin gh-pages
cd .. && rm -rf dist/.git
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

{"action":"add","body":"## IWSDK Daily Build (PM Cycle) — Initial Planning & Build

This is the noon kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch. This is the AFTERNOON cycle — a separate build from the overnight one.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json` throughout. (Note: PM cycle uses `-pm.json`, not the midnight cycle's file.)

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "cycle": "pm",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends**, update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build.","enabled":true,"id":"iwsdk-daily-build-pm","mode":"task","schedule":{"kind":"daily","time":"12:00:00","timezone":"America/Los_Angeles"},"timeout_secs":25200}

summary:
Context checkpoint compaction summary:
# Context Checkpoint: IWSDK Dev Skill + Galaga VR

## Who
Felix — IWSDK framework author at Meta. Building VR/MR/browser-first 3D apps with IWSDK (Immersive Web SDK). GitHub user: `ellyz2426`.

## What happened this session

### 1. IWSDK Dev Skill Update (COMPLETED)
Felix provided his original IWSDK dev skill (written for Manus agents, ~v0.3.2) and asked me to modernize it for v0.4.1 and make it generic for all cloud-based AI harnesses (not Manus-specific, not Hatch-specific).

**I wrote a first draft** → Felix reviewed it and provided a corrected version with significant factual fixes. **His version is the canonical one**, now saved at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`.

**Key corrections Felix made to my draft:**
- `--no-xr` does NOT auto-wire browser locomotion/mouselook — must add `browserControls: true` manually
- `ecs step` uses `"count"` not `"frames"`; `ecs find` uses `"withComponents"` not `"components"`
- `set-device-state` wraps in a `"state"` key: `{"state":{"controllers":{"right":{...}}}}`
- IWER now normalizes device name aliases (my claim they "all fail" was outdated)
- `@iwsdk/reference` build failure — `--ignore-scripts` is wrong workaround (bin expects built dist)
- Dual-runtime pattern is simpler: just `xr: { offer: 'once' }` with `browserControls: true`
- Use `InputComponent` typed API (`.Trigger`, `.Squeeze`) not raw gamepad indices
- Node engine range: `>=20.19.0 <21.0.0-0 || >=22.12.0 <23.0.0-0 || >=24.0.0`
- Camera default height is 1.7, not 1.6
- CLI-first operating model rationale (MCP adapters unreliable in cloud harnesses)

### 2. Galaga VR — End-to-End XR Controller Support (COMPLETED)
Felix flagged that the game didn't work with VR controllers alone. I audited all game states and found 6 gaps, spawned a subagent that fixed them all.

**Repo:** `~/workspace/galaga-vr/` → deployed at `https://ellyz2426.github.io/galaga-vr/`

**Fixes applied (7 files, +1667 lines):**
1. **Settings menu** — added `handleXRNav()` for full thumbstick/trigger/B navigation
2. **Stats screen** — XR dismiss via trigger/B
3. **Boss intro** — added `skipBossIntro()` to game.ts, wired to trigger
4. **Pause overlay** — shows "Press B to resume" in VR mode
5. **Homing/mega blast** — accept optional aim params from controller ray
6. **Double-fire prevention** — `xrManaged` flag stops ShootingSystem from independently firing in XR
7. **Title screen access** — A button opens settings, thumbstick-down opens stats
8. **XRInputManager** — added `menuLeft`/`menuRight` state from thumbstick X

**Verified:** Build hash `index-tyAVHFY1.js` matches between local dist and deployed gh-pages. All XR patterns present in minified output.

## Key environment details
- Node 20.19.0 at `/tmp/node-v20.19.0-linux-x64/bin/`
- IWSDK 0.4.1 (packages at 0.4.0 with post-release patches)
- IWSDK monorepo at `~/workspace/immersive-web-sdk/`
- Three.js imports from `@iwsdk/core`, never from `three`
- GitHub auth via `gh` CLI as `ellyz2426`

## IWSDK Daily Build — Initial Planning & Build

This is the midnight kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends** (whether you finish or get cut off), update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers. Use IWSDK's action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages** — push built dist to `gh-pages` branch:
   ```
   npm run build
   cd dist && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
   git push -f origin gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope — do not try to push .github/workflows files.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file as described above, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Include screenshots in `.agent/journal/state/`. Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build. Build as much as you can in this round.

You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.\n\n### Before anything else:\n\n1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`\n2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.\n\n### Status file protocol:\n\nYou MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.\n\n**At the start**, write:\n```json\n{\n  \"schema_version\": 1,\n  \"date\": \"YYYY-MM-DD\",\n  \"game_name\": \"<game-name>\",\n  \"game_dir\": \"/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>\",\n  \"repo_url\": \"https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>\",\n  \"pages_url\": \"https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/\",\n  \"build_start_utc\": \"<ISO timestamp>\",\n  \"target_hours\": 6,\n  \"total_build_minutes\": 0,\n  \"status\": \"building\",\n  \"rounds\": [],\n  \"features_completed\": [],\n  \"last_updated_utc\": \"<ISO timestamp>\"\n}\n```\n\n**When your build round ends** (whether you finish or get cut off), update the status file:\n- Set `status` to `\"waiting_continuation\"` (if under 6h) or `\"complete\"` (if ≥6h)\n- Append to `rounds` array: `{\"round\": N, \"start_utc\": \"...\", \"end_utc\": \"...\", \"minutes\": N, \"files\": N, \"lines\": N, \"features_added\": [...]}`\n- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)\n- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far\n- Set `last_updated_utc`\n\n### Build instructions:\n\n1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.\n\n2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:\n   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)\n   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)\n   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.\n   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime\n   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)\n   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish\n\n3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:\n   ```\n   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description \"<description>\"\n   ```\n\n4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages** — push built dist to `gh-pages` branch:\n   ```\n   npm run build\n   PROJECT=\"$PWD\"\n   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy\n   git init && cp -R \"$PROJECT/dist/.\" .\n   git add -A && git commit -m \"Deploy\"\n   git push --force \"https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git\" HEAD:gh-pages\n   ```\n   Then configure Pages:\n   ```\n   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f \"build_type=legacy\" -F \"source[branch]=gh-pages\" -F \"source[path]=/\"\n   ```\n   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope — do not try to push .github/workflows files.\n\n5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.\n\n6. **When your round ends**, update the status file as described above, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.\n\n7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Include screenshots in `.agent/journal/state/`. Commit and push.\n\nHourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build. Build as much as you can in this round.","enabled":true,"id":"iwsdk-daily-build","mode":"task","schedule":{"kind":"daily","time":"00:00:00","timezone":"America/Los_Angeles"},"timeout_secs":25200}","call_id":"toolu_01SoEyZhQ9QMPskuKCtDEoX6","name":"cron","type":"function_call"},{"call_id":"toolu_01SoEyZhQ9QMPskuKCtDEoX6","output":"{"changed_paths":["/home/hatch/workspace/cron.d/daily/iwsdk-daily-build__daily@00:00:00.md"],"id":"iwsdk-daily-build","path":"/home/hatch/workspace/cron.d/daily/iwsdk-daily-build__daily@00:00:00.md","schedule_key":"daily@00:00:00","scheduler_sync":"reconciled"}","success":true,"type":"function_call_output"},{"role":"assistant","text":"Now fix the sentinel jobs. ## IWSDK Daily Build — Morning Summary & Validation

This is the final wrap-up job. Three responsibilities:

### 1. Deliver morning briefing

Read `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` and `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`.

Deliver a concise summary including:
- Game name and one-line concept
- Live URL (GitHub Pages) and repo URL
- Key features (5-8 bullets)
- Scale: file count and line count
- Total build time (accumulated across all rounds)
- Number of rounds it took

### 2. Validate `.agent/journal/` in GitHub

Check that the build journal exists in the repo:
```
gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/contents/.agent/journal --jq '.[].name' 2>&1
```

If missing or incomplete:
- Find session JSONL files from `~/agents/` that correspond to today's build
- Copy them into the game's `.agent/journal/` directory
- Create `.agent/journal/state/` if it doesn't exist
- Commit and push

### 3. Validate GitHub Pages deployment

```
curl -sI https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/ | head -3
```

If not returning HTTP 200:
- Rebuild: `cd ~/workspace/<game-name> && npm run build`
- Redeploy to gh-pages branch
- Trigger pages build: `gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages/builds -X POST`
- Verify it comes up

### 4. Mark build complete

Update `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`:
- Set `status` to `"complete"` regardless (the build window is over)
- This prevents any straggler check-in jobs from starting new rounds

Deliver to main chat and Telegram.
","success":true,"type":"function_call_output"},{"arguments":"{"path":"workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/checkin-protocol.md"}","call_id":"toolu_01DhdiV7p1dgPvvCbULJ1UFd","name":"read","type":"function_call"},{"call_id":"toolu_01DhdiV7p1dgPvvCbULJ1UFd","output":"## IWSDK Build Check-in — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job. Your job is to check whether the overnight IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → A build agent is currently running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The midnight job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if ≥6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
cd dist && rm -rf .git && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
git push -f origin gh-pages
cd .. && rm -rf dist/.git
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

(Note: PM cycle uses `-pm.json`, not the midnight cycle's file.)\n\n**At the start**, write:\n```json\n{\n  \"schema_version\": 1,\n  \"date\": \"YYYY-MM-DD\",\n  \"cycle\": \"pm\",\n  \"game_name\": \"<game-name>\",\n  \"game_dir\": \"/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>\",\n  \"repo_url\": \"https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>\",\n  \"pages_url\": \"https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/\",\n  \"build_start_utc\": \"<ISO timestamp>\",\n  \"target_hours\": 6,\n  \"total_build_minutes\": 0,\n  \"status\": \"building\",\n  \"rounds\": [],\n  \"features_completed\": [],\n  \"last_updated_utc\": \"<ISO timestamp>\"\n}\n```\n\n**When your build round ends**, update the status file:\n- Set `status` to `\"waiting_continuation\"` (if under 6h) or `\"complete\"` (if ≥6h)\n- Append to `rounds` array: `{\"round\": N, \"start_utc\": \"...\", \"end_utc\": \"...\", \"minutes\": N, \"files\": N, \"lines\": N, \"features_added\": [...]}`\n- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)\n- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far\n- Set `last_updated_utc`\n\n### Build instructions:\n\n1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.\n   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime\n   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)\n   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish\n\n3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:\n   ```\n   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description \"<description>\"\n   ```\n\n4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:\n   ```\n   npm run build\n   PROJECT=\"$PWD\"\n   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy\n   git init && cp -R \"$PROJECT/dist/.\" .\n   git add -A && git commit -m \"Deploy\"\n   git push --force \"https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git\" HEAD:gh-pages\n   ```\n   Then configure Pages:\n   ```\n   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f \"build_type=legacy\" -F \"source[branch]=gh-pages\" -F \"source[path]=/\"\n   ```\n   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.\n\n5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.\n\n6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md`.\n\n7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). item.name: cron
item.arguments:
{"action":"add","body":"## IWSDK Daily Build (PM Cycle) — Evening Summary & Validation

This is the final wrap-up job for the afternoon build cycle. Three responsibilities:

### 1. Deliver evening briefing

Read `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md` and `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`.

Deliver a concise summary including:
- Game name and one-line concept
- Live URL (GitHub Pages) and repo URL
- Key features (5-8 bullets)
- Scale: file count and line count
- Total build time (accumulated across all rounds)
- Number of rounds it took

### 2. Validate `.agent/journal/` in GitHub

Check that the build journal exists in the repo:
```
gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/contents/.agent/journal --jq '.[].name' 2>&1
```

If missing or incomplete:
- Find session JSONL files from `~/agents/` that correspond to today's PM build
- Copy them into the game's `.agent/journal/` directory
- Create `.agent/journal/state/` if it doesn't exist
- Commit and push

### 3. Validate GitHub Pages deployment

```
curl -sI https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/ | head -3
```

If not returning HTTP 200:
- Rebuild: `cd ~/workspace/<game-name> && npm run build`
- Redeploy to gh-pages branch
- Trigger pages build: `gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages/builds -X POST`
- Verify it comes up

### 4. Mark build complete

Update `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`:
- Set `status` to `"complete"` regardless (the build window is over)
- This prevents any straggler check-in jobs from starting new rounds

Deliver to main chat and Telegram.","delivery":[{"surface":"main"},{"surface":"telegram"}],"enabled":true,"id":"iwsdk-daily-build-summary-pm","mode":"task","schedule":{"kind":"daily","time":"20:30:00","timezone":"America/Los_Angeles"}}

<task_context>
## IWSDK Daily Build (PM Cycle) — Initial Planning & Build

This is the noon kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch. This is the AFTERNOON cycle — a separate build from the overnight one.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json` throughout. (Note: PM cycle uses `-pm.json`, not the midnight cycle's file.)

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "cycle": "pm",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends**, update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build.
</task_context>
<worker_message>
**IWSDK PM Build — Round 1 Complete: Holo Golf VR** 🏌️

New game built and deployed from scratch in ~26 minutes:

- **Live:** https://ellyz2426.github.io/holo-golf/
- **Repo:** https://github.com/ellyz2426/holo-golf
- **Stats:** 21 source files, 4,423 lines, 4 commits, 4 deploys

**What it is:** Futuristic holographic mini golf in VR — neon wireframe courses suspended in holodeck space.

**Features built:**
- 18 holes across 2 full courses (Neon Circuit + Quantum Field)
- 5 obstacle types: windmills, bumpers, moving walls, ramps, spinners
- Custom ball physics (velocity, friction, wall bounce, hole capture)
- XR controller swing detection + browser click-drag putting (dual runtime)
- Holodeck environment: neon grid, 2000-star starfield, floating geometry
- Procedural audio via Web Audio API (ambient drone, putt/swing/bounce SFX, arpeggios)
- 12 achievements with persistent tracking + popup notifications
- Play statistics, power meter, detailed scorecard (TAB), hole intro banners
- Course select with best score persistence

**Status:** `waiting_continuation` — hourly check-in jobs can continue enhancing. Build log, summary, and journal all written. MEMORY.md updated with build #5 in the registry.
</worker_message>

**Holo Golf VR — PM Build Round 2 complete (1 PM check-in)**

Continued the Holo Golf build (status was `waiting_continuation` at 26 min). Round 2 ran ~11 min, bringing total to 37 min / 360 target. Status reset to `waiting_continuation`.

**Round 2 changes (commit fb76c89, deployed):**
- **Course 3 "Cosmic Abyss"** — 9 expert holes with teleporters, wind zones, ice surfaces (27 holes total across 3 courses)
- **Bumper collision physics** — balls now bounce off bumpers with 1.8x velocity boost + new audio
- **Special obstacles fully integrated** — teleporter warps (cooldown + purple particle burst), wind zone directional force, ice surface friction override
- **Camera orbit fix** — was completely non-functional (empty `update()` method); now tracks ball, right-drag orbits, scroll zooms
- **Scorecard fix** — dynamically reads hole names from active course data (was hardcoded to course 1 only)
- **3 new sound effects** — bumper hit, teleport whoosh, wind zone ambient
- **Ball trail optimized** — object pool replaces per-frame geometry allocation
- All type errors fixed for IWSDK 0.3.x/0.4.x API compatibility

**Stats:** 22 files, 5,156 lines (+733). 5 deploys total.
**Live:** https://ellyz2426.github.io/holo-golf/

Note: Node 20.19.0 had to be re-downloaded to `/tmp/` (was cleared since last session). Build requires Node ≥20.19.0 for Vite 7.x `crypto.hash`.
</worker_message>

Holo Golf VR keeps growing — 1 PM sentinel added a third course and fixed several issues:

- **Course 3 "Cosmic Abyss"** — 9 expert holes with teleporters, wind zones, and ice surfaces (27 holes total now)
- Fixed camera orbit (was completely broken), scorecard rendering, and bumper collision physics
- 3 new sound effects, ball trail performance optimization
- **22 files, 5,156 lines** (+733 from round 1)

Still `waiting_continuation` at 37 min — plenty of room for the 2–7 PM sentinels to keep building.

This is the final wrap-up job for the afternoon build cycle. Three responsibilities:

### 1. Deliver evening briefing

Read `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md` and `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`.

Deliver a concise summary including:
- Game name and one-line concept

- Key features (5-8 bullets)
- Scale: file count and line count
- Total build time (accumulated across all rounds)
- Number of rounds it took

### 2. Validate `.agent/journal/` in GitHub

Check that the build journal exists in the repo:
```
gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/contents/.agent/journal --jq '.[].name' 2>&1
```

If missing or incomplete:
- Find session JSONL files from `~/agents/` that correspond to today's PM build
- Copy them into the game's `.agent/journal/` directory
- Create `.agent/journal/state/` if it doesn't exist
- Commit and push

### 3. Validate GitHub Pages deployment

```
curl -sI https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/ | head -3
```

If not returning HTTP 200:
- Rebuild: `cd ~/workspace/<game-name> && npm run build`
- Redeploy to gh-pages branch
- Trigger pages build: `gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages/builds -X POST`
- Verify it comes up

### 4. Mark build complete

Update `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`:
- Set `status` to `"complete"` regardless (the build window is over)
- This prevents any straggler check-in jobs from starting new rounds

Deliver to main chat and Telegram.

## IWSDK Daily Build — Initial Planning & Build

This is the midnight kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends** (whether you finish or get cut off), update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages** — push built dist to `gh-pages` branch:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope — do not try to push .github/workflows files.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file as described above, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Include screenshots in `.agent/journal/state/`. Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build. Build as much as you can in this round.
</task_context>

**Holodeck 10-pin bowling in VR + browser dual-runtime.** Second consecutive successful automated overnight build.

🔗 **Live:** https://ellyz2426.github.io/astro-bowl/
📦 **Repo:** https://github.com/ellyz2426/astro-bowl

- **VR controller input** — grip grab, trigger throw with velocity tracking, thumbstick nav
- **Power-up system** — 8 types with inventory HUD, earned through gameplay

- **Full settings** — audio, controls, accessibility (3 colorblind modes), graphics

---
name: iwsdk-dev
description: >
  MUST read this skill BEFORE developing VR/MR/browser-first 3D applications
  using IWSDK (Immersive Web SDK). Covers project scaffolding, headless
  browser setup, CLI tools, reference system, ECS debugging, XR emulation,
  verification workflows, deployment, and known issues.
---

# IWSDK Development Guide

**Current version: 0.4.1** · [iwsdk.dev](https://iwsdk.dev) · [GitHub](https://github.com/facebook/immersive-web-sdk)

This guide is for AI agents and developers building WebXR or browser-first 3D
applications with IWSDK in cloud/headless Linux environments (no physical GPU,
no display server). It assumes Node.js satisfies IWSDK's package engine range:
`>=20.19.0 <21.0.0-0 || >=22.12.0 <23.0.0-0 || >=24.0.0`.
The managed dev browser uses Playwright Chromium; the dev plugin installs the
matching Chromium binary when it is missing.

### CLI-First Operating Model

This guide is intentionally **CLI-first** for cloud-based harnesses. Treat
`npx iwsdk ...` commands as the primary control surface for setup, runtime
inspection, screenshots, ECS debugging, XR emulation, and reference queries.

Reason: MCP adapter support in cloud harnesses is often missing, stale, or
partially wired. Relying on MCP tool availability can degrade generation quality
because the agent may plan around tools that are not actually connected. Use MCP
tools only when the harness clearly exposes them and they are already working;
otherwise use the CLI commands in this guide. Do not rewrite CLI examples into
MCP-only workflows.

---

## 1. Project Scaffolding

Always use the official `@iwsdk/create` package. Use `--yes` to bypass
interactive prompts.

**Do not hardcode scaffolding flags.** Infer the correct flags from the
request. When ambiguous, ask.

### Hard Constraints (Always Apply)

- **`--no-metaspatial`** — use the manual workflow in cloud/headless Linux.
  Meta Spatial Editor authoring requires the macOS/Windows GUI; the Linux CLI is
  for CI/CD build workflows, not GUI authoring.

### Step 1: XR vs Browser-First

OK, I now understand the architecture. IWSDK has a built-in spatial UI system based on `@pmndrs/uikit` and `@pmndrs/uikitml`:

1. **PanelUI component** — renders `.uikitml` templates (HTML-like syntax) as 3D spatial panels
2. **ScreenSpace component** — positions panels in screen-space when not in XR, automatically returns to world space when in XR
3. **UIKitDocument** — DOM-like wrapper with getElementById, querySelector, etc.
4. **Vite plugin** — `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml` compiles `.uikitml` files to JSON


5. **Follow component** — handles head-locked UI positioning

The critical realization is that HTML DOM overlays won't render in immersive XR mode, so all UI—menus, HUDs, scorecards—needs to be built with spatial PanelUI and `.uikitml` templates instead. ## 5. Dev Server

```bash

npx iwsdk dev up --foreground  # Stay attached to terminal

npx iwsdk dev logs           # View recorded background server logs
npx iwsdk dev open           # Open in browser
```

managed headless Playwright Chromium, registers the MCP WebSocket endpoint, and
records runtime state. Use `npx iwsdk dev status` for the resolved `localUrl`;
the generated starter template defaults to `https://localhost:8081/`, but
examples or existing apps may use another Vite port.

## IWSDK Daily Build — Initial Planning & Build

This is the midnight kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends** (whether you finish or get cut off), update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode (`{ xr: false }`) for dual runtime
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages** — push built dist to `gh-pages` branch:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope — do not try to push .github/workflows files.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file as described above, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Include screenshots in `.agent/journal/state/`. Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build. Build as much as you can in this round.

This is the midnight kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md` — especially section **4b (Spatial UI / PanelUI)**.
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` throughout.

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],

}
```

**When your build round ends**, update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**ALL game UI MUST use IWSDK's built-in PanelUI system. Do NOT use HTML DOM elements for any game UI.**

This is a hard gate. HTML `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, or any DOM overlay for menus, HUDs, scorecards, settings, notifications, or any other game UI is **forbidden**. HTML overlays are invisible in XR and are an anti-pattern.

Instead, use:
- **`.uikitml` templates** in a `ui/` directory, compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- **`PanelUI` component** to attach compiled UI panels to entities
- **`Follower` component** with `world.player.head` target for head-locked HUDs in XR
- **`ScreenSpace` component** for browser-mode overlays that auto-return to world-space in XR
- **World-space panels** for in-scene menus, leaderboards, settings boards

Read the IWSDK dev skill section **4b** for full API details, examples, and patterns.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features or adding content.**

Build order for every game:
1. Core gameplay loop + basic XR controller input
2. All UI as PanelUI spatial panels (menus, HUD, scorecards, settings)
3. **VERIFY in XR:** Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
   - All panels render and are readable in XR
   - XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
   - HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
   - No HTML overlays remain
4. ONLY THEN expand with additional features, content, modes, polish

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **ALL UI via PanelUI** — `.uikitml` templates, `Follower` for HUDs, `ScreenSpace` for overlays. Zero HTML DOM UI.
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode with dual runtime (`xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `browserControls: true`)
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"

   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

item.name: cron
item.arguments:
{"action":"update","body":"## IWSDK Daily Build (PM Cycle) — Initial Planning & Build

This is the noon kick-off job. You start a fresh IWSDK game build from scratch. This is the AFTERNOON cycle — a separate build from the overnight one.

### Before anything else:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md` — especially section **4b (Spatial UI / PanelUI)**.
2. **Check the build log** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` — DO NOT repeat any concept already listed there.

### Status file protocol:

You MUST maintain `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json` throughout. (Note: PM cycle uses `-pm.json`, not the midnight cycle's file.)

**At the start**, write:
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "cycle": "pm",
  "game_name": "<game-name>",
  "game_dir": "/home/hatch/workspace/<game-name>",
  "repo_url": "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>",
  "pages_url": "https://ellyz2426.github.io/<game-name>/",
  "build_start_utc": "<ISO timestamp>",
  "target_hours": 6,
  "total_build_minutes": 0,
  "status": "building",
  "rounds": [],
  "features_completed": [],
  "last_updated_utc": "<ISO timestamp>"
}
```

**When your build round ends**, update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**ALL game UI MUST use IWSDK's built-in PanelUI system. Do NOT use HTML DOM elements for any game UI.**

This is a hard gate. HTML `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, or any DOM overlay for menus, HUDs, scorecards, settings, notifications, or any other game UI is **forbidden**. HTML overlays are invisible in XR and are an anti-pattern.

Instead, use:
- **`.uikitml` templates** in a `ui/` directory, compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- **`PanelUI` component** to attach compiled UI panels to entities
- **`Follower` component** with `world.player.head` target for head-locked HUDs in XR
- **`ScreenSpace` component** for browser-mode overlays that auto-return to world-space in XR
- **World-space panels** for in-scene menus, leaderboards, settings boards

Read the IWSDK dev skill section **4b** for full API details, examples, and patterns.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features or adding content.**

Build order for every game:
1. Core gameplay loop + basic XR controller input
2. All UI as PanelUI spatial panels (menus, HUD, scorecards, settings)
3. **VERIFY in XR:** Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
   - All panels render and are readable in XR
   - XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
   - HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
   - No HTML overlays remain
4. ONLY THEN expand with additional features, content, modes, polish

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **ALL UI via PanelUI** — `.uikitml` templates, `Follower` for HUDs, `ScreenSpace` for overlays. Zero HTML DOM UI.
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode with dual runtime (`xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `browserControls: true`)
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build.","enabled":true,"id":"iwsdk-daily-build-pm","mode":"task","schedule":{"kind":"daily","time":"12:00:00","timezone":"America/Los_Angeles"},"timeout_secs":25200}

{"new_text":"## IWSDK Build Check-in — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job. Your job is to check whether the overnight IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → A build agent is currently running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The midnight job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**Before expanding features, audit existing code for HTML DOM UI anti-patterns.**

Search the codebase for: `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `.style.`, `document.body`, `getElementById` on `document` (not on `UIKitDocument`). If any game UI uses HTML DOM overlays, **fix them first** by converting to `.uikitml` PanelUI panels before adding new features.

All game UI must use IWSDK's PanelUI system:
- `.uikitml` templates compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- `PanelUI` component for 3D panels
- `Follower` component for head-locked HUDs
- `ScreenSpace` component for browser overlays that auto-adapt in XR
- Read section **4b** of the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features.**

Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
- All PanelUI panels render and are readable in XR
- XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
- HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
- No HTML overlays remain in the codebase

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.
